summary: A transient, in-memory REST service managing multiple resource collections: Users, Organizations, Buildings, Cars, Bicycles, and Office Tables. Each resource type supports create/get/list/update/delete operations, uses system-generated identifiers, enforces simple per-resource domain validation, and persists only for the lifetime of the process.

groups.name: User Management
groups.summary: Capabilities for creating and fully replacing User resources with a fixed id/name/email schema, UUID generation, and email uniqueness enforced in an in-memory store.

requirements.title: Create User endpoint
requirements.statement: The system SHALL expose a REST endpoint that creates a User from a supplied name and email.

acceptance.given: a creation request containing valid values for name and email
acceptance.outcome: a new User is persisted with a system-generated UUID as its id, and a success response is returned

acceptance.given: a creation request that does not include a name
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request as invalid

acceptance.given: a creation request that does not include an email
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request as invalid

acceptance.given: a creation request that includes an email already held by a persisted User
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request to preserve email uniqueness

acceptance.given: a creation request that includes extra fields beyond name and email
acceptance.outcome: the persisted User contains only id, name, and email; any additional fields are discarded

requirements.title: Replace User endpoint
requirements.statement: The system SHALL expose a REST endpoint that fully replaces an existing persisted User with a complete User record.

acceptance.given: an existing User and a replace request containing a complete User record including id, name, and email
acceptance.outcome: the stored User is fully replaced with the supplied record, and a success response is returned

acceptance.given: a replace request referencing an id that does not identify any persisted User
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request because the target User does not exist

acceptance.given: a replace request for an existing User where the request body is missing name, email, or id
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request as invalid

acceptance.given: a replace request where the id in the request body does not match the targeted User's id
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request as invalid

acceptance.given: a replace request for an existing User that repeats the User's own current email
acceptance.outcome: the replacement succeeds and the User remains uniquely represented

acceptance.given: a replace request for an existing User that specifies an email already held by a different User
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request to preserve email uniqueness

acceptance.given: a replace request for an existing User that includes extra fields beyond id, name, and email
acceptance.outcome: the persisted User contains only id, name, and email; any additional fields are discarded

requirements.title: UUID identifier generation
requirements.statement: The system SHALL generate a UUID for the User id upon creation and SHALL NOT allow clients to supply or modify it.

acceptance.given: a User creation request
acceptance.outcome: the id value is a valid, system-generated UUID

acceptance.given: two independent User creation requests
acceptance.outcome: each User is assigned a distinct id

acceptance.given: a creation request that includes a client-supplied id
acceptance.outcome: the system ignores the supplied id and assigns its own UUID

acceptance.given: an existing User
acceptance.outcome: the User's id remains unchanged from the value assigned at creation

requirements.title: Email uniqueness
requirements.statement: The system SHALL enforce that the email field is unique across all persisted Users for both creation and replacement operations.

acceptance.given: a User already exists with a specific email address
acceptance.when: a creation request specifies that same email
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request

acceptance.given: a User already exists with a specific email address
acceptance.when: a replace request for a different User specifies that same email
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request

acceptance.given: a User already exists with a specific email address
acceptance.when: a replace request for that same User repeats the current email
acceptance.outcome: the replacement succeeds and uniqueness is maintained

acceptance.given: no persisted User has a specific email address
acceptance.when: a creation or replace request specifies that email
acceptance.outcome: the operation succeeds and the new email is stored

requirements.title: User representation
requirements.statement: The system SHALL represent a User with exactly the fields id, name, and email, and SHALL NOT persist additional fields supplied by clients.

acceptance.given: any persisted User
acceptance.outcome: it contains exactly the fields id, name, and email

acceptance.given: a User has been created or replaced
acceptance.outcome: no extra computed or metadata fields are present beyond id, name, and email

requirements.title: In-memory persistence
requirements.statement: The system SHALL use an in-memory store for all User persistence.

acceptance.given: a User has been persisted
acceptance.when: the running process serves subsequent requests
acceptance.outcome: the User record remains available and can be replaced within the same process instance

acceptance.given: a User has been persisted
acceptance.outcome: the previously persisted User records are no longer present

groups.name: Organization Management
groups.summary: Capabilities for creating and fully replacing Organization resources with a fixed id/name/slug schema, UUID generation, and slug uniqueness enforced in an in-memory store.

requirements.title: Create Organization endpoint
requirements.statement: The system SHALL expose a REST endpoint that creates an Organization from a supplied name and slug.

acceptance.given: a creation request containing valid values for name and slug
acceptance.outcome: a new Organization is persisted with a system-generated UUID as its id, and a success response is returned

acceptance.given: a creation request that does not include a name
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request as invalid

acceptance.given: a creation request that does not include a slug
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request as invalid

acceptance.given: a creation request that includes a slug already held by a persisted Organization
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request to preserve slug uniqueness

acceptance.given: a creation request that includes a slug not matching the required lowercase-alphanumeric-hyphen format
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request as invalid

acceptance.given: a creation request that includes extra fields beyond name and slug
acceptance.outcome: the persisted Organization contains only id, name, and slug; any additional fields are discarded

requirements.title: Replace Organization endpoint
requirements.statement: The system SHALL expose a REST endpoint that fully replaces an existing persisted Organization with a complete Organization record.

acceptance.given: an existing Organization and a replace request containing a complete Organization record including id, name, and slug
acceptance.outcome: the stored Organization is fully replaced with the supplied record, and a success response is returned

acceptance.given: a replace request referencing an id that does not identify any persisted Organization
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request because the target Organization does not exist

acceptance.given: a replace request for an existing Organization where the request body is missing id, name, or slug
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request as invalid

acceptance.given: a replace request where the id in the request body does not match the targeted Organization's id
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request as invalid

acceptance.given: a replace request for an existing Organization that repeats the Organization's own current slug
acceptance.outcome: the replacement succeeds and slug uniqueness is maintained

acceptance.given: a replace request for an existing Organization that specifies a slug already held by a different Organization
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request to preserve slug uniqueness

acceptance.given: a replace request for an existing Organization that includes a slug not matching the required lowercase-alphanumeric-hyphen format
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request as invalid

acceptance.given: a replace request for an existing Organization that includes extra fields beyond id, name, and slug
acceptance.outcome: the persisted Organization contains only id, name, and slug; any additional fields are discarded

requirements.title: Organization UUID identifier generation
requirements.statement: The system SHALL generate a UUID for the Organization id upon creation and SHALL NOT allow clients to supply or modify it.

acceptance.given: an Organization creation request
acceptance.outcome: the id value is a valid, system-generated UUID

acceptance.given: two independent Organization creation requests
acceptance.outcome: each Organization is assigned a distinct id

acceptance.given: a creation request that includes a client-supplied id
acceptance.outcome: the system ignores the supplied id and assigns its own UUID

acceptance.given: an existing Organization
acceptance.outcome: the Organization's id remains unchanged from the value assigned at creation

requirements.title: Slug uniqueness
requirements.statement: The system SHALL enforce that the slug field is unique across all persisted Organizations for both creation and replacement operations.

acceptance.given: an Organization already exists with a specific slug
acceptance.when: a creation request specifies that same slug
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request

acceptance.given: an Organization already exists with a specific slug
acceptance.when: a replace request for a different Organization specifies that same slug
acceptance.outcome: the system rejects the request

acceptance.given: an Organization already exists with a specific slug
acceptance.when: a replace request for that same Organization repeats the current slug
acceptance.outcome: the replacement succeeds and uniqueness is maintained

acceptance.given: no persisted Organization has a specific slug
acceptance.when: a creation or replace request specifies that slug
acceptance.outcome: the operation succeeds and the new slug is stored

requirements.title: Organization representation
requirements.statement: The system SHALL represent an Organization with exactly the fields id, name, and slug, and SHALL NOT persist additional fields supplied by clients.

acceptance.given: any persisted Organization
acceptance.outcome: it contains exactly the fields id, name, and slug

acceptance.given: an Organization has been created or replaced
acceptance.outcome: no extra computed or metadata fields are present beyond id, name, and slug

requirements.title: Organization in-memory persistence
requirements.statement: The system SHALL use an in-memory store for all Organization persistence.

acceptance.given: an Organization has been persisted
acceptance.when: the running process serves subsequent requests
acceptance.outcome: the Organization record remains available and can be replaced within the same process instance

acceptance.given: an Organization has been persisted
acceptance.outcome: the previously persisted Organization records are no longer present

groups.name: Building Management
groups.summary: Capabilities for creating, retrieving, updating, deleting, and listing Building resources with unique identifiers, name and address fields, and cursor-based pagination over a transient in-memory store.

requirements.title: Create Building endpoint
requirements.statement: The system SHALL expose a REST API endpoint to create an office building from a name and an address.

acceptance.given: a creation request containing a name not exceeding 80 characters and an address not exceeding 160 characters
acceptance.outcome: the system stores the Building with a unique identifier and returns the Building record

acceptance.given: a creation request containing a name exceeding 80 characters
acceptance.outcome: the system performs basic error handling and does not create the Building

acceptance.given: a creation request containing an address exceeding 160 characters
acceptance.outcome: the system performs basic error handling and does not create the Building

acceptance.given: a creation request containing a missing name or address
acceptance.outcome: the system performs basic error handling and does not create the Building

requirements.title: Retrieve Building endpoint
requirements.statement: The system SHALL expose a REST API endpoint to retrieve an office building by its unique identifier.

acceptance.given: a Building exists for the provided identifier
acceptance.outcome: the system returns the Building record

acceptance.given: no Building exists for the provided identifier
acceptance.outcome: the system performs basic error handling

requirements.title: Update Building endpoint
requirements.statement: The system SHALL expose a REST API endpoint to update an existing office building.

acceptance.given: a stored Building and a request containing a name not exceeding 80 characters and an address not exceeding 160 characters
acceptance.outcome: the system modifies the record and returns the updated Building

acceptance.given: no Building exists for the provided identifier
acceptance.outcome: the system performs basic error handling

acceptance.given: an update request containing a name exceeding 80 characters
acceptance.outcome: the system performs basic error handling and does not modify the Building

acceptance.given: an update request containing an address exceeding 160 characters
acceptance.outcome: the system performs basic error handling and does not modify the Building

requirements.title: Delete Building endpoint
requirements.statement: The system SHALL expose a REST API endpoint to permanently delete an office building by its unique identifier.

acceptance.given: a stored Building
acceptance.outcome: the system permanently removes the record from the in-memory store

acceptance.given: no Building exists for the provided identifier
acceptance.outcome: the system performs basic error handling

requirements.title: List Buildings endpoint
requirements.statement: The system SHALL expose a REST API endpoint to list office buildings with cursor-based pagination.

acceptance.given: zero stored buildings
acceptance.outcome: the system returns a paginated collection with no items

acceptance.given: fewer buildings than the page size
acceptance.outcome: the system returns all buildings and a pagination response indicating no next cursor

acceptance.given: more buildings than the page size
acceptance.when: the list endpoint is invoked with a page size parameter
acceptance.outcome: the system returns a single page of buildings and a next cursor for retrieving subsequent buildings

acceptance.given: a valid next cursor from a previous list response
acceptance.when: the list endpoint is invoked with that cursor
acceptance.outcome: the system returns the next page of buildings

requirements.title: Building representation
requirements.statement: The system SHALL represent a Building with exactly the fields id, name, and address, and SHALL NOT persist additional fields supplied by clients.

acceptance.given: any persisted Building
acceptance.outcome: it contains exactly the fields id, name, and address

requirements.title: Building in-memory persistence
requirements.statement: The system SHALL store all Building data in memory only, without disk persistence, snapshotting, or file-based survival, and SHALL initialize the store empty on startup.

acceptance.given: a Building has been persisted
acceptance.outcome: no Building data from the previous process lifecycle remains

acceptance.given: the service has just started
acceptance.outcome: the store contains no seed data

groups.name: Car Management
groups.summary: Capabilities for typical CRUD operations on Car resources using a transient in-memory store.

requirements.title: Car CRUD endpoints
requirements.statement: The system SHALL expose REST endpoints to create, retrieve, list, update, and delete Car resources, using an in-memory store.

acceptance.given: a valid create request for a Car
acceptance.outcome: the Car is persisted with a system-generated unique identifier and returned

acceptance.given: a stored Car and a valid identifier
acceptance.outcome: the system returns the Car record

acceptance.given: a Car identifier that does not exist
acceptance.outcome: the system performs error handling indicating the resource was not found

acceptance.given: one or more stored Cars and a list request
acceptance.outcome: the system returns a collection of Car records with cursor-based pagination

acceptance.given: a stored Car and a valid full-update request
acceptance.outcome: the system replaces the stored Car fields and returns the updated record

acceptance.given: a non-existent Car identifier on update
acceptance.outcome: the system performs error handling indicating the resource was not found

acceptance.given: a stored Car identifier
acceptance.outcome: the system permanently removes the Car from the in-memory store

acceptance.given: a non-existent Car identifier on delete
acceptance.outcome: the system performs error handling indicating the resource was not found

requirements.title: Car identifier and representation
requirements.statement: The system SHALL generate a unique identifier for each Car upon creation and represent a Car with a fixed set of fields.

acceptance.given: a Car creation request
acceptance.outcome: the id value is a valid, system-generated identifier

acceptance.given: two independent Car creation requests
acceptance.outcome: each Car is assigned a distinct id

acceptance.given: any persisted Car
acceptance.outcome: it contains the expected fixed fields and no extra computed fields

requirements.title: Car in-memory persistence
requirements.statement: The system SHALL persist all Car data in memory only, with an empty store on startup and no data survival across restarts.

acceptance.given: a Car has been persisted
acceptance.outcome: no Car data from the previous process lifecycle remains

groups.name: Bicycle Management
groups.summary: Capabilities for typical CRUD operations on Bicycle resources using a transient in-memory store.

requirements.title: Bicycle CRUD endpoints
requirements.statement: The system SHALL expose REST endpoints to create, retrieve, list, update, and delete Bicycle resources, using an in-memory store.

acceptance.given: a valid create request for a Bicycle
acceptance.outcome: the Bicycle is persisted with a system-generated unique identifier and returned

acceptance.given: a stored Bicycle and a valid identifier
acceptance.outcome: the system returns the Bicycle record

acceptance.given: a Bicycle identifier that does not exist
acceptance.outcome: the system performs error handling indicating the resource was not found

acceptance.given: one or more stored Bicycles and a list request
acceptance.outcome: the system returns a collection of Bicycle records with cursor-based pagination

acceptance.given: a stored Bicycle and a valid full-update request
acceptance.outcome: the system replaces the stored Bicycle fields and returns the updated record

acceptance.given: a non-existent Bicycle identifier on update
acceptance.outcome: the system performs error handling indicating the resource was not found

acceptance.given: a stored Bicycle identifier
acceptance.outcome: the system permanently removes the Bicycle from the in-memory store

acceptance.given: a non-existent Bicycle identifier on delete
acceptance.outcome: the system performs error handling indicating the resource was not found

requirements.title: Bicycle identifier and representation
requirements.statement: The system SHALL generate a unique identifier for each Bicycle upon creation and represent a Bicycle with a fixed set of fields.

acceptance.given: a Bicycle creation request
acceptance.outcome: the id value is a valid, system-generated identifier

acceptance.given: two independent Bicycle creation requests
acceptance.outcome: each Bicycle is assigned a distinct id

acceptance.given: any persisted Bicycle
acceptance.outcome: it contains the expected fixed fields and no extra computed fields

requirements.title: Bicycle in-memory persistence
requirements.statement: The system SHALL persist all Bicycle data in memory only, with an empty store on startup and no data survival across restarts.

acceptance.given: a Bicycle has been persisted
acceptance.outcome: no Bicycle data from the previous process lifecycle remains

groups.name: Office Table Management
groups.summary: Capabilities for creating, retrieving, listing, updating, and deleting Office Table resources with UUIDv7 identifiers, non-negative price validation, fixed id/price/date-bought representation, full-replacement updates, direct JSON payloads, cursor-based pagination, and no API version prefixes.

requirements.title: Office Table CRUD endpoints
requirements.statement: The system SHALL expose REST endpoints for Create, Read, Update, and Delete operations on office table resources; exact URL paths SHALL be determined at implementation time.

acceptance.given: the service is running
acceptance.outcome: it exposes RESTful endpoints supporting all CRUD operations for office tables

requirements.title: Office Table representation
requirements.statement: The system SHALL represent each office table resource with the fields id, price, and date bought, generating id as a UUIDv7 upon creation.

acceptance.given: a request to create an office table
acceptance.outcome: the system assigns a UUIDv7 identifier and stores the provided price and date bought

acceptance.given: any stored office table
acceptance.outcome: its JSON representation contains id, price, and date bought

acceptance.given: a creation request that includes a client-supplied id
acceptance.outcome: the system ignores the supplied id and assigns its own UUIDv7 identifier

requirements.title: Office Table price validation
requirements.statement: The system SHALL reject any create or update request that specifies a negative price.

acceptance.given: a request payload with a negative price
acceptance.when: the create endpoint processes the request
acceptance.outcome: the system returns an HTTP status code consistent with common REST conventions indicating a client validation error and does not persist the invalid resource

acceptance.given: a request payload with a negative price for an existing office table
acceptance.when: the update endpoint processes the request
acceptance.outcome: the system returns an HTTP status code consistent with common REST conventions indicating a client validation error and does not persist the invalid resource

acceptance.given: a request payload with a zero price
acceptance.when: the create or update endpoint processes the request
acceptance.outcome: the operation succeeds because zero is a non-negative price

requirements.title: Retrieve Office Table endpoint
requirements.statement: The system SHALL provide an endpoint to retrieve a single office table by its UUIDv7 identifier.

acceptance.given: an existing office table UUIDv7 identifier
acceptance.outcome: the system returns the resource directly as JSON with an HTTP status code consistent with common REST conventions for a successful retrieval

acceptance.given: a non-existent UUIDv7 identifier
acceptance.outcome: the system returns an HTTP status code consistent with common REST conventions indicating the resource was not found

requirements.title: List Office Tables endpoint
requirements.statement: The system SHALL provide an endpoint to list office tables with cursor-based pagination.

acceptance.given: multiple office tables exist in the store
acceptance.outcome: the system returns a paginated collection directly as JSON using cursor-based pagination

requirements.title: Office Table full replacement update
requirements.statement: The system SHALL support only full resource replacement for updates.

acceptance.given: an existing office table
acceptance.when: an update request is received with a payload
acceptance.outcome: the system replaces all stored fields with the payload contents and returns the updated resource

requirements.title: Direct JSON payloads
requirements.statement: The system SHALL return resource and collection payloads directly as JSON without an envelope wrapper, using HTTP status codes consistent with common REST conventions.

acceptance.given: any successful CRUD operation for office tables
acceptance.outcome: the body contains the resource or collection directly without a data envelope

requirements.title: Office Table in-memory persistence
requirements.statement: The system SHALL persist office table resources in a fully ephemeral in-memory store that is cleared on service restart.

acceptance.given: the service has stored office tables
acceptance.outcome: no office table data from the previous process lifecycle remains

requirements.title: No API version prefixes
requirements.statement: The system SHALL NOT include API version prefixes in endpoint URLs.

acceptance.given: any endpoint URL for office tables
acceptance.outcome: it does not contain a version path segment such as /v1/

rules.rule: A User id is immutable and is system-assigned as a UUID at creation time.
rules.rationale: Guarantees unique identity without requiring client coordination and prevents identity spoofing or alteration.

rules.rule: The email address acts as a unique key across the entire user population.
rules.rationale: Prevents duplicate accounts and ambiguous identity lookups.

rules.rule: Replacement of a User is a full-record operation requiring id, name, and email; partial updates are not permitted.
rules.rationale: Avoids partial-update ambiguity and keeps the state transfer contract explicit.

rules.rule: A User entity has exactly the fields id, name, and email.
rules.rationale: Keeps the domain model minimal and predictable for clients.

rules.rule: User data persists only in memory for the lifetime of the process; no durable storage or cross-process replication is provided.
rules.rationale: Matches the explicit in-memory store requirement and sets clear durability expectations.

rules.rule: An Organization id is immutable and is system-assigned as a UUID at creation time.
rules.rationale: Guarantees unique identity without requiring client coordination and prevents identity spoofing or alteration.

rules.rule: The slug field acts as a unique key across the entire Organization population and must match the lowercase-alphanumeric-hyphen pattern.
rules.rationale: Provides a human-readable unique identifier while preventing ambiguous or malformed slugs.

rules.rule: Replacement of an Organization is a full-record operation requiring id, name, and slug; partial updates are not permitted.
rules.rationale: Avoids partial-update ambiguity and keeps the state transfer contract explicit.

rules.rule: An Organization entity has exactly the fields id, name, and slug.
rules.rationale: Keeps the domain model minimal and predictable for clients.

rules.rule: Organization data persists only in memory for the lifetime of the process; no durable storage or cross-process replication is provided.
rules.rationale: Matches the explicit in-memory store requirement and sets clear durability expectations.

rules.rule: A Building entity has exactly the fields id, name, and address.
rules.rationale: Schema defined by the product owner.

rules.rule: A Building name must not exceed 80 characters and a Building address must not exceed 160 characters.
rules.rationale: Input validation rules provided by the product owner.

rules.rule: No uniqueness constraints apply to the Building name or address fields.
rules.rationale: The product owner stated there are no uniqueness constraints beyond the identifier.

rules.rule: Deletion of a Building permanently removes it from the in-memory store.
rules.rationale: The product owner specified hard delete.

rules.rule: Building data persists only in memory for the lifetime of the process.
rules.rationale: The product owner specified fully transient data.

rules.rule: A Car entity has a fixed set of fields and a system-generated unique identifier, and persists only in memory for the lifetime of the process.
rules.rationale: Ensures a predictable transient model for simple CRUD.

rules.rule: A Bicycle entity has a fixed set of fields and a system-generated unique identifier, and persists only in memory for the lifetime of the process.
rules.rationale: Ensures a predictable transient model for simple CRUD.

rules.rule: An Office Table id is an immutable UUIDv7 generated by the system at resource creation; client-provided ids are not accepted.
rules.rationale: Provides time-ordered, globally unique identifiers while preventing identity spoofing.

rules.rule: An Office Table price must be non-negative for every persisted resource.
rules.rationale: Prevents invalid financial values for furniture records.

rules.rule: An Office Table entity has exactly the fields id, price, and date bought.
rules.rationale: Keeps the furniture resource model minimal and predictable for clients.

rules.rule: Replacement of an Office Table is a full replacement of all stored fields; partial updates via PATCH are prohibited.
rules.rationale: Avoids partial-update ambiguity and keeps the state transfer contract explicit.

rules.rule: Office Table data persists only in memory for the lifetime of the process.
rules.rationale: Matches the explicit fully ephemeral in-memory store requirement.

rules.rule: Office Table endpoint URLs SHALL NOT contain API version path segments.
rules.rationale: The product owner specified no API version prefixes.
